title: Ibn Khaldun - The Father of Sociology
text: Ibn Khaldun (1332-1406) was a pioneering historian, sociologist, and economist. Born in Tunis, he is best known for his work 'Muqaddimah' (Introduction to History), which laid the foundations for several social sciences. His theories on social cohesion, economic principles, and historical methodology were centuries ahead of their time and influenced later European thinkers.
